bulk.name: Mulch
bulk.blurb: Most beds do well with 2–3 inches of mulch. Use 3 inches for weed suppression, 2 inches around annuals and shallow-rooted plants, and keep mulch a couple of inches away from trunks and stems.
bulk.tip: Bulk mulch by the cubic yard is usually much cheaper than bags once you need more than about 1 yard (13–14 bags).

bulk.name: Topsoil
bulk.blurb: New garden beds typically need 4–6 inches of topsoil, raised-bed topping 2–4 inches, and lawn leveling only ½–1 inch. Topsoil is heavy — about 1.1 tons per cubic yard — so plan delivery for anything over a yard.
bulk.tip: For lawn top-dressing at ½ inch, divide the ½-inch numbers from the 1-inch figure — or use the calculator with a custom depth.

bulk.name: Compost
bulk.blurb: For amending beds, spread 1–3 inches of compost and work it into the top 6 inches of soil. For lawn top-dressing, ¼–½ inch is plenty.
bulk.tip: Compost compresses — if it will sit in a pile for weeks before spreading, order about 10% extra.

bulk.name: Gravel
bulk.blurb: Walkways and decorative areas need 2–3 inches of gravel; driveways need 4 inches or more, usually in two compacted layers over a base. Gravel weighs about 1.4 tons per cubic yard.
bulk.tip: For driveways, order crushed stone with fines (e.g. #57 over a crusher-run base) rather than round pea gravel, which never locks in place.

bulk.name: River Rock
bulk.blurb: Use rock at least twice as deep as its largest stone size: 2 inches for small (¾–1 inch) rock, 3–4 inches for larger stones. Lay landscape fabric underneath to keep rock from sinking into soil.
bulk.tip: River rock is sold by the ton as often as by the yard — the weight figures below are what the quarry will quote.

bulk.name: Sand
bulk.blurb: Paver leveling beds use 1 inch of sand, sandboxes 2–3 inches (or more), and lawn leveling ¼–½ inch at a time. Sand weighs about 1.35 tons per cubic yard.
bulk.tip: Use coarse washed sand (paver/concrete sand) for bases and leveling — play sand is too fine and holds water.
